trends.name: Iran Military Escalation & Oil Markets
trends.summary: US military strikes on Iran's Kharg Island and escalating Middle East conflict (embassy attacks, missile interceptions) are driving immediate geopolitical risk premiums. Oil prices rising sharply with emergency strategic petroleum reserve releases failing to contain upward pressure, creating stagflation concerns for equities.

trends.name: Defense Stocks as Safe Haven Play
trends.summary: Escalating Middle East tensions driving capital flows into defense sector ETFs as investors seek geopolitical hedges. 15 S&P 500 stocks already showing double-digit gains since Iran conflict began, positioning defense as the new market safe haven.

trends.name: Tech Sector Weakness & Mag7 Underperformance
trends.summary: Magnificent 7 index unchanged since August and down 9% YTD, signaling major tech fatigue despite AI hype. Rotation away from mega-cap tech accelerating as investors flee expensive valuations, with non-tech S&P 500 outperforming significantly.

trends.name: Meta Layoffs & Tech Workforce Disruption
trends.summary: Meta planning 20%+ layoffs signals broader tech industry contraction despite AI spending surge. Represents structural shift in employment with AI replacing jobs faster than anticipated, creating macro headwinds for consumer spending.

trends.name: Unprecedented ETF Trading Activity
trends.summary: US ETF weekly volume hit $2.2 trillion (2nd highest on record), 215% above 2023-2024 average, indicating retail investor mobilization. Gen Z showing highest risk appetite (80% in speculative assets) due to feeling financially behind, creating dangerous bubble dynamics.

trends.name: Fed Policy & FOMC Decision Impact
trends.summary: Upcoming FOMC meeting taking center stage as geopolitical risk, inflation concerns from oil shock, and labor market weakness (unemployment rising) create conflicting pressures on rate policy. Market facing potential lost decade scenario if yields remain elevated.

trends.name: Income/Dividend Stock Rotation Accelerating
trends.summary: Significant rotation into high-yield dividend and preferred stocks (7-13% yields mentioned) as investors flee growth stocks. Sector rotation benefiting dividend aristocrats and income-focused ETFs amid tech underperformance and market uncertainty.

trends.name: Berkshire Hathaway Share Buybacks Resume
trends.summary: Berkshire Hathaway filing signals resumption of aggressive share repurchases, major signal from Buffett that equity valuations attractive despite market concerns. Demonstrates institutional confidence contrasting with retail panic and market technicals deterioration.

trends.name: Bitcoin Outperformance in Geopolitical Crisis
trends.summary: Bitcoin significantly outperforming S&P 500, Nasdaq, and gold since Iran conflict began, attracting crisis-driven capital flows. Digital assets positioning as geopolitical hedge alongside traditional safe havens, signaling loss of confidence in legacy markets.

trends.name: Oil Shock Broadens Economic Weakness
trends.summary: Oil prices surging to October 2023 highs ($3.67 gasoline) amid emergency stockpile releases failing, broadening inflationary pressure on consumer spending. Weekly indicators showing oil shock deepening across energy and transportation sectors with structural supply concerns.

trends.name: Market Valuation & Earnings Yield Crisis
trends.summary: S&P 500 earnings yield below 4% facing competition from 6%+ fixed income yields, creating fundamental valuation pressure. Multiple signals of technical weakness, with financial sector showing 'spooky' technical patterns suggesting structural market vulnerability.

trends.name: Disposable Income Surge vs. Cost Pressures
trends.summary: Real disposable income surged fastest in 3 years on temporary tax benefits, but masking underlying pressure: gas, rent, groceries all rising with unemployment increasing. Consumer resilience artificial and temporary, creating dangerous false signal for Fed policy.
